name: illuminati-writing-doctrine-rules
purpose: Operational rules derived from the embedded corpus for high-discipline intelligence writing.

rules.title: Judgment First
rules.action: State the main judgment in the title or first paragraph.

rules.title: Audience Vector
rules.action: Infer or state the consumer, decision, and time horizon before structuring the draft.
rules.failure_signal: The draft contains information but no decision value.

rules.title: Evidence Ranking
rules.action: Rank evidence by relevance, reliability, and impact on the judgment.
rules.failure_signal: Facts appear as inventory rather than support.

rules.title: Judgment-Evidence Separation
rules.action: Keep known facts, assessed conclusions, and unknowns distinct.
rules.failure_signal: The draft blurs observation and analysis.

rules.title: Competing Hypothesis Check
rules.action: Identify the strongest rival explanation and address why it loses or remains viable.
rules.failure_signal: One explanation is presented as inevitable without contest.

rules.title: Confidence Discipline
rules.action: Assign confidence based on source quality, corroboration, and reasoning strength.

rules.title: Core Assertion Paragraphs
rules.action: Begin each paragraph with a point, judgment, or topic sentence.
rules.failure_signal: Paragraphs begin with setup or vague transition.

rules.title: Inverted Pyramid Flow
rules.trigger: Any explanatory or assessment body section
rules.action: Move from the highest-value point to supporting detail.
rules.failure_signal: The most important implication arrives late.

rules.title: Active Voice Bias
rules.action: Prefer active voice unless the object is more important or the actor is unknown.
rules.failure_signal: Passive phrasing hides agency or weakens the sentence.

rules.title: Compression
rules.action: Cut redundancy, inflated phrases, and formal throat-clearing.
rules.failure_signal: The sentence is longer than its analytic value.

rules.title: No Data Dump
rules.action: Convert collections of facts into meaning, implication, or ranked support.
rules.failure_signal: The draft accumulates detail without significance.

rules.title: Bias Exposure
rules.action: Name relevant source bias, acquisition-chain distortion, or corroboration limits.
rules.failure_signal: Weakly sourced claims are presented as settled.

rules.title: Implication Requirement
rules.action: Explain why the assessment matters now and what follows from it.
rules.failure_signal: The reader learns facts but not significance.

rules.title: Cold Tone
rules.failure_signal: The draft sounds sentimental, melodramatic, or self-impressed.

rules.title: No Sci-Fi Gimmicks
rules.action: Make the prose feel superior through logic and compression, not supernatural claims.
rules.failure_signal: The draft sounds like fiction rather than analysis.

rules.title: Forward Watchpoints
rules.action: Name indicators, triggers, or watchpoints that would strengthen, weaken, or reverse the judgment.
rules.failure_signal: The product stops at present-state description.

rules.title: Format as Contract
rules.action: Ensure the title and section headers match the actual judgment delivered.
rules.failure_signal: The heading promises more or something different than the body supports.

rules.title: Cyber Tradecraft Caveat
rules.action: Use estimative language, source validation, and operational implications.
rules.failure_signal: Cyber claims sound absolute without tradecraft caveats.
